title: GOOD: Lock-free atomic check in lichen_lora_l2_tx avoids 500ms mutex starvation
description: lora_l2.c lines 326-339: TX uses atomic_get(running) without the mutex because lora_send() blocks for ~500ms at SF10/255B. Holding lora_mutex would starve the RX thread's callback pointer snapshot. The code correctly documents why this is safe (atomic read + driver-internal serialization). This is not 'cutting corners' - it's deliberate avoidance of a real deadlock risk.
